Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Limiting The Number Of Requests For Admission And Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Chance Pardon
Off Calendar Mar-27-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF NEREIDA CRUZ', AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion For A Protective Order Limiting The Number Of Requests For Admission And Specially Prepared Interrogatories.

Plaintiff's motion for a protective order limiting the number of special interrogatories and requests for admission is DENIED as moot. In support of the opposition, defense counsel Christopher Maloney declares that Defendants withdrew special interrogatories (SPROGs) and requests for admission (RFAs) so that each Defendant is propounding no more than the statutory floor. This moots the issues raised in the motion. To the extent Plaintiff wanted the Court to limit all three Defendants to 35 cumulative requests/interrogatories, that request is denied. Each party can propound 35 RFAs/SPROGs. (CCP Section 2033.030; CCP Section 2030.030(a)(1).)

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
